IN HER CAUSES OF ACTION FOR DECLARATORY RELIEF AND PROMISSORY ESTOPPEL PLAINTIFF IS TO (1) IDENTIFY THE RIGHTS TO BE ADJUDICATED VIS-A-VIS THE ASSOCIATION ONLY, FOR WHICH RELIEF MAY BE GRANTED WITHOUT ANY IMPACT ON THE OWNERSHIP RIGHTS OF OTHER MEMBERS, I.E. THAT ASSOCIATION HAS OWNERSHIP OF THE ROOF OR THAT ASSOCIATION'S ACTIONS OR INACTIONS ARE BINDING ON THE TRUE OWNERS; OR (2) IF PLAINTIFF CANNOT IN GOOD FAITH ALLEGE THAT ASSOCIATION IS THE OWNER, PLAINTIFF SHALL NAME OTHER MEMBERS AS ACTUAL DEFENDANTS, NOT NOMINAL DEFENDANTS. DEMURRER TO THE CAUSE OF ACTION FOR BREACH OF FIDUCIARY DUTY AGAINST THE ASSOCIATION IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. THIS CAUSE OF ACTION WAS INCLUDED WITHOUT LEAVE OF COURT. ADDITIONALLY, PLAINTIFF MAKES NO SUBSTANTIVE OPPOSITION TO THE DEMURRER. MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Eric Sidebotham, esq. (408-267-4500) of Parr Law Group for Plaintiff/opposing party Fahima Zahir via Court Call.
Maria Kao, esq. (925-838-2090) of Berding Weil for Defendant/Moving party 18 LANSING STREET OWNERS'S ASSOCIATION.

The Court announced the tentative ruling. Discussed re: clarification cause of action as to Directors only, fiduciary duty against the association is sustained without leave to amend. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT 18 LANSING STREET OWNERS'S ASSOCIATION's DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED with final leave to amend. In her causes of action for declaratory relief and promissory estoppel Plaintiff is to (1) identify the rights to be adjudicated vis-a-vis the Association only, for which relief may be granted without any impact on the ownership rights of other members, i.e. that Association has ownership of the roof or that Association's actions or inactions are binding on the true owners; or (2) if Plaintiff cannot in good faith allege that Association is the owner, Plaintiff shall name other members as actual defendants, not nominal defendants. Demurrer to the cause of action for breach of fiduciary duty against the Association is sustained without leave to amend. This cause of action was included without leave of Court. Additionally, Plaintiff makes no substantive opposition to the demurrer. Plaintiff's improper request (made in opposition to the demurrer) for leave to amend the complaint to allege a new cause of action for breach of fiduciary duties against the directors is DENIED. Prevailing party to submit order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Judge: Ronald E. Quidachay, Clerk: S. Le, Not Reported. (D501)

Appearances: Paul Windust of Berding Weil LLP (925.838.2090) for defendant and moving party; Eric J. Sidebotham of the Parr Law Group (408.267.4500) appearing via Court Call for plaintiff and responding party.

Argued and the Court modifies its ruling as follows: SUSTAINED with leave to amend. The Court notes that Plaintiff asserts at least 5 different theories upon which her "rights" to the decks are based within causes of action for declaratory relief and promissory estoppel. The co-owners of the common areas for which Plaintiff seeks a declaration of rights, are necessary and indispensable parties as the declaration of rights is sought, inter alia, based on prescriptive easements, and equitable easement, easement by estoppel and irrevocable parol license. Plaintiff shall have 30 days leave to amend to include other HOA members as defendants as to both causes of action to the extent referenced above The issue of indispensable parties has not been reached on prior demurrers. Prevailing party to submit a proposed form of order in compliance with CRC 3.1312. Judge: Suzanne Ramos Bolanos, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Not Reported (501/SRB)
